technology.title: Should Social Media Be Banned for Under-16s?
technology.description: Take a stance on age limits for social media and argue whether protecting young users outweighs their right to access online communities.
questions: Argue FOR: Do the mental-health risks justify a hard age limit?
Argue AGAINST: Does a ban push teens toward less-safe, unregulated spaces?

technology.title: AI Writing Tools Belong in the Classroom
technology.description: Defend or challenge the position that students should be allowed to use AI writing assistants for schoolwork.
questions: What is your thesis: is AI a calculator for writing, or a shortcut around learning?
How do you answer the strongest objection to your view?

technology.title: Smartphones Have No Place in Schools
technology.description: Write a persuasive editorial arguing for or against a full phone ban during the school day.
questions: Argue FOR: Do phones harm focus and worsen bullying enough to justify a ban?
Argue AGAINST: Are phones essential safety and learning tools?

technology.title: In Defense of the Right to Disconnect
technology.description: Make the case that workers should have a legal right to ignore work messages outside working hours.
questions: What concrete change are you urging readers or lawmakers to support?
Why is voluntary etiquette not enough to fix the problem?

technology.title: No, Video Games Are Not Making Kids Violent
technology.description: A rebuttal editorial answering the common claim that gaming causes real-world aggression.
questions: What is the opposing claim you are answering, stated fairly?
What evidence best dismantles that claim?

social.title: Voting Should Be Mandatory
social.description: Argue for or against compulsory voting and what it would mean for a healthy democracy.
questions: Argue FOR: Does higher turnout produce more legitimate government?
Argue AGAINST: Is forcing a vote a violation of personal freedom?

social.title: It's Time to Rethink How We Treat the Homeless
social.description: A call-to-action editorial urging your community to adopt a specific policy on homelessness.
questions: What single action are you asking readers to demand or support?
How do you counter the 'it's too expensive' objection?

social.title: Cancel Culture Does More Harm Than Good
social.description: State and defend your opinion on public shaming and accountability online.
questions: Where do you draw the line between accountability and mob justice?
What is the strongest case against your position?

social.title: The Minimum Wage Should Be a Living Wage
social.description: Take a persuasive stance on tying the minimum wage to the real cost of living.
questions: Argue FOR: Is a living wage a matter of basic fairness?
Argue AGAINST: Would it cost jobs and raise prices for everyone?

social.title: Why Community Service Should Be a Graduation Requirement
social.description: Persuade your school board to require volunteer hours for a diploma.
questions: What specific requirement are you proposing?
How do you respond to students who say it forces charity?

environment.title: Ban Single-Use Plastics Now
environment.description: A call-to-action editorial pressing for an immediate ban on disposable plastics in your town or country.
questions: What exact ban or policy are you demanding, and from whom?
How do you answer businesses that say the switch is too costly?

environment.title: Nuclear Power Is the Green Energy We're Afraid Of
environment.description: Argue for or against expanding nuclear energy as a climate solution.
questions: Argue FOR: Is nuclear the fastest path to cutting emissions?
Argue AGAINST: Do waste and safety risks outweigh the benefits?

environment.title: Individual Recycling Won't Save the Planet
environment.description: A rebuttal challenging the idea that personal habits, not corporate policy, are the real fix for climate change.
questions: What comforting belief are you pushing back against?
Where should responsibility actually sit, and why?

environment.title: Cities Should Be Built for People, Not Cars
environment.description: Defend the opinion that urban design must prioritise walking, cycling and transit.
questions: What does a people-first city look like in one sentence?
How do you answer drivers who feel punished by the change?

environment.title: Fast Fashion Is a Crisis We Keep Ignoring
environment.description: Persuade readers to change how they buy clothes, or to demand industry regulation.
questions: What are you urging readers to do differently starting today?
Why isn't 'just buy less' a complete answer?

health.title: Junk Food Ads Aimed at Kids Should Be Banned
health.description: Argue for or against restricting advertising of unhealthy food to children.
questions: Argue FOR: Do children deserve protection from manipulative marketing?
Argue AGAINST: Is this the government overreaching into parents' choices?

health.title: Mental Health Days Should Count as Sick Days
health.description: A call-to-action editorial urging schools or employers to recognise mental health absences.
questions: What policy change are you specifically asking for?
How do you answer the fear that people will abuse it?

health.title: We're Wrong About How We Talk About Diets
health.description: A rebuttal challenging popular but harmful assumptions about weight and health.
questions: What widely repeated claim are you correcting?
What should replace that thinking?

health.title: Vaccines Should Be Required for School Entry
health.description: Take a firm persuasive stance on mandatory childhood vaccination.
questions: Argue FOR: Does community protection justify a mandate?
Argue AGAINST: Where does personal medical freedom fit?

health.title: Why Schools Must Teach Real Sleep Habits
health.description: Persuade educators that later start times and sleep education would improve student wellbeing.
questions: What is your core claim about teens and sleep?
How do you rebut 'earlier starts build discipline'?

education.title: Standardised Tests Measure the Wrong Things
education.description: State and defend your opinion on whether high-stakes testing should be scrapped.
questions: What do tests actually reward, in your view?
What is the strongest argument in favour of keeping them?

education.title: Homework Should Be Abolished in Primary School
education.description: Argue for or against ending homework for young children.
questions: Argue FOR: Does homework harm young kids more than it helps?
Argue AGAINST: Does it build habits that matter later?

education.title: College Isn't the Only Path Worth Respecting
education.description: A rebuttal to the assumption that university is the default route to a good life.
questions: What cultural assumption are you challenging?
What alternatives deserve equal respect, and why?

education.title: Every School Should Teach Personal Finance
education.description: A call-to-action editorial demanding financial literacy be added to the curriculum.
questions: What specifically should schools be required to teach?
How do you answer 'there's no room in the timetable'?

education.title: Grading Systems Are Failing Students
education.description: Defend the opinion that letter grades should be replaced with better feedback.
questions: What is broken about the current grade, in your view?
What would you replace it with, and how do you defend that?

current.title: Remote Work Is Here to Stay, and That's a Good Thing
current.description: Take a persuasive stance on whether the shift away from the office should be embraced or reversed.
questions: What is your one-line verdict on remote work?
How do you answer bosses who say culture needs the office?

current.title: The News Media Is Breaking Our Trust
current.description: Argue for or against the claim that modern journalism has lost the public's confidence.
questions: Argue FOR: Has sensationalism damaged the press beyond repair?
Argue AGAINST: Is 'fake news' talk itself the real threat?

current.title: It's Time to Take a Four-Day Week Seriously
current.description: A call-to-action editorial urging employers or lawmakers to trial a shorter work week.
questions: What action are you asking readers to support?
How do you rebut 'less time means less output'?

current.title: We Shouldn't Fear Immigration, We Should Plan for It
current.description: Defend a considered opinion on how societies should respond to migration.
questions: What is your central claim about immigration?
What is the fairest version of the opposing view, and your reply?

current.title: The Space Race Is a Distraction From Earth's Problems
current.description: A rebuttal challenging the idea that private space exploration deserves its current hype.
questions: What popular enthusiasm are you pushing back on?
Where do you think that money and attention should go?

media.title: Influencers Should Be Held to Advertising Standards
media.description: A call-to-action editorial demanding clearer rules for paid promotion online.
questions: What specific rule or disclosure are you calling for?
How do you answer creators who say it stifles free expression?

media.title: Streaming Has Made Us Worse at Paying Attention
media.description: Defend or challenge the opinion that endless content is reshaping how we think.
questions: What is your claim about attention and streaming?
What is the best counterargument, and your response?

media.title: Don't Blame Video Games for Society's Problems
media.description: A rebuttal answering critics who scapegoat entertainment media for real-world issues.
questions: What accusation are you answering, stated fairly?
What is the real cause you'd point to instead?

ethics.title: Zoos Do More Harm Than Good
ethics.description: Argue for or against keeping animals in zoos in the modern era.
questions: Argue FOR: Is captivity indefensible even for conservation?
Argue AGAINST: Do zoos protect species that would otherwise vanish?

ethics.title: Genetic Editing of Humans Crosses a Line
ethics.description: State and defend your opinion on the ethics of editing the human genome.
questions: Where exactly do you think the ethical line falls?
How do you answer parents who could prevent disease this way?

ethics.title: Anonymous Online Speech Deserves Protection
ethics.description: Argue for or against the right to speak anonymously on the internet.
questions: Argue FOR: Does anonymity protect whistleblowers and the vulnerable?
Argue AGAINST: Does it shield harassment and lies?
